name: NAS Jacksonville
tagline: The East Coast hub of naval aviation patrol, flying the P-8 Poseidon, in a big, affordable Florida Navy town.
_note: Navy air station (Jacksonville, FL; part of a large Navy metro with NS Mayport). Facts verified from official sources (cnrse.cnic.navy.mil, jacksonville.tricare.mil, Balfour Beatty, DeCA/NEX, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). BAH MHA FL058 (Jacksonville) via the bah_rates single source of truth. Housing operated by Balfour Beatty. Florida is TRICARE East. Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx), representative off-base ZIP 32244 (Jacksonville).

data.Mission: East Coast hub of maritime patrol and reconnaissance aviation
data.Aircraft: P-8A Poseidon and helicopter maritime squadrons
data.Nearby: Part of a large Navy metro with NS Mayport
data.Setting: Northeast Florida; no state income tax

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Naval Air Station Jacksonville is one of the Navy's largest air stations and the East Coast hub for maritime patrol and reconnaissance, flying the P-8A Poseidon, along with helicopter maritime strike and sea combat squadrons.
It anchors a large Navy presence in northeast Florida that also includes Naval Station Mayport, making 'Jax' one of the Navy's biggest concentrations on the East Coast.

data.subtitle: Jacksonville is a big, relatively affordable city with no state income tax and beaches close by. Where you live depends on which base you report to (NAS Jax or Mayport) and your commute.
intro: Jacksonville sprawls, so the metro offers a wide range of prices and neighborhoods, and Florida's lack of a state income tax stretches your pay. The Atlantic beaches are a draw on the east side.
Your installation pulls you toward one side of the river: NAS Jax is on the Westside/Orange Park side, while Mayport sits out by the beaches. Pick your area around your daily commute.

recommendations.advice: Orange Park and the Westside (Clay and Duval County) are close to the air station and affordable.
The beaches communities (Atlantic, Neptune, Jacksonville Beach) suit families at Mayport, at a higher price.
St. Johns County (south of the city) is highly rated for schools, at a longer commute.
On-base housing (Balfour Beatty) is available; weigh it against the affordable local market and your commute.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Visitors without a DoD ID need a visitor pass and a REAL ID-compliant ID from the Visitor Control Center.
Gate hours and visitor requirements change, so confirm the current details on the official NAS Jacksonville site before you go.

sections.title: On-Base Housing
data: On-base military family housing is privatized, operated by Balfour Beatty Communities, serving NAS Jacksonville and the area's other Navy installations.
Eligibility and availability vary by rank and family size, so start with the Navy housing office and the partner for current options and waitlists.

sections.title: Schools
data: There is no DoDEA district here; children attend the local civilian public schools. Duval County serves most of Jacksonville; Clay County (Orange Park) and the highly rated St. Johns County (to the south) are popular with families.
Your assigned schools depend on your address, so confirm the district and campus for any neighborhood you are considering.

data.Care: Inpatient, outpatient, surgical, and intensive care, with branch clinics including one at Mayport

intro: Whether to rent or buy is the biggest money decision of most PCS moves, and the answer is mostly about how long you'll be here and how steady the local market is.
rent: Your tour is short (about two years or less) or you expect to be at sea often.
You want time to learn the Jacksonville metro before committing.
You'd rather not gamble on resale timing when you PCS out.
Your BAH comfortably covers rent in the areas you'd consider.
buy: You expect to be here three or more years.
You want to use your VA loan and build equity instead of paying rent.
The relatively affordable market and no state income tax fit your plan.
You've found a neighborhood and commute you're confident about.
data.bottom_line: There is no universal right answer. Jacksonville is relatively affordable for a big coastal city, helped by no state income tax, though factor flood and hurricane insurance near the water. Run your BAH against the rent and home prices above, weigh your tour length, and talk to a lender and an agent who work with military families before you commit.

intro: The first month at a new duty station is mostly logistics. Knocking these out early makes everything else easier.

steps.title: Line up temporary lodging
steps.text: Book Navy Gateway Inns or a short-term rental before you arrive. You can claim Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) for a limited number of days while you house-hunt.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your command and finance, and make sure DEERS and ID cards are current for every family member so TRICARE and base access work.

steps.title: Start with the Housing Office
steps.text: Check on-base availability and get the housing office's read on the Jacksonville metro before you sign anything.

steps.title: Register your vehicle and sort access
steps.text: Handle base access and Florida vehicle registration, and learn the visitor-pass process for guests. Bring your registration and insurance.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Contact the gaining district (Duval, Clay, or St. Johns) early and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Update your TRICARE enrollment so you can be seen at Naval Hospital Jacksonville or network providers.

data.a: It's the East Coast hub for maritime patrol aviation, flying the P-8A Poseidon, plus helicopter maritime squadrons. The Jacksonville area also includes Naval Station Mayport.
Orange Park and the Westside are closest to NAS Jax; the beaches suit Mayport families; St. Johns County is popular for schools. On-base housing is also available.
Relatively, for a big coastal city, and Florida has no state income tax. Factor flood and hurricane insurance for homes near the water.
NAS Jacksonville is MHA FL058 (Jacksonville). Use the calculator above for your grade.

picks.name: Jacksonville Beaches
picks.blurb: Atlantic, Neptune, and Jacksonville Beach on the east side of the city.

picks.name: St. Augustine
picks.blurb: The nation's oldest city, with a historic Spanish quarter, about an hour south.

picks.name: Jacksonville Zoo and Gardens
picks.blurb: A large zoo and botanical gardens on the city's north side.
